observable_summary: Minimal Chihara-like Al observable: backend free-electron DSF plus an explicit central elastic/ion-feature proxy from the bound-electron form factor; bound/core inelastic term is kept explicit and currently zero in the narrow article benchmark window. Bound/core inelastic term was set to zero because the narrow Al benchmark window stays below the first L-shell onset, so this layer currently keeps only the free-electron term and the central elastic/ion-feature proxy.
warnings: Plasmon electron policy 'Article Al benchmark' is active: Article Al benchmark active-subset materials: Al@elements.Al(cold_Zeff=3.000, mode=cold_baseline_plus_increment) -> baseline 3.000 + increment 0.000 = 3.000; unresolved [none]; raw-kept [none]. This mode is intended for literature-facing benchmark comparisons, not as a blind replacement for raw hydro ionization. || Benchmark spectral mode uses a refined odd energy grid (>= 4001 points) and local quadratic peak extraction; advanced invalid states remain hard failures instead of fallbacks. || Theta = Te/Ef < 1, so the selected state is at least partially degenerate; the current spectral branch still uses the classical finite-temperature susceptibility baseline and should be treated as approximate in degenerate WDM conditions. || The selected non-LFC spectral branch ignores the requested static-LFC backend setting (esa_static). Choose a dedicated static-LFC plasmon model if you want correlation corrections applied. || LOS-integrated spectral mode sums 1000 active zones through 1 cached plasmon state clusters before the instrument-response convolution.
